operating_state: Total revenues increased 16% to $12.2 billion driven by NIKE Brand growth and Converse expansion. NIKE Direct revenues grew 28% representing 40% of total NIKE Brand revenues, with digital commerce reaching $2.5 billion. Regional performance showed broad strength with double-digit growth across North America, EMEA, and APLA, while Greater China delivered solid momentum despite supply constraints.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 170 basis points to 46.5% primarily driven by higher NIKE Direct margins, favorable product mix shifts, and reduced promotional activity, partially offset by elevated freight costs reducing gross margin by approximately 120 basis points. Selling and administrative expenses increased 20% with demand creation expense rising 36% due to increased brand campaign spending and digital marketing investments, while operating overhead grew from higher wage-related costs and strategic technology investments.
material_events: The company resumed share repurchases with $752 million in buybacks during the quarter under the four-year $15 billion program and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.275 per share. Factory closures in Vietnam and Indonesia due to COVID-19 resulted in approximately ten weeks of lost production affecting 51% of NIKE Brand footwear and 30% of apparel production. The Brazil operations transition to distributor model was completed while Argentina, Chile and Uruguay entities remain classified as held-for-sale.
macro_exposures: Supply chain disruptions intensified with government-mandated factory shutdowns in Vietnam and Indonesia creating production constraints across key categories. Extended inventory transit times worsened due to port congestion, transportation delays, and container shortages, resulting in elevated logistics costs and product availability constraints. Foreign currency translation provided a favorable $104 million impact on pre-tax income despite strengthening dollar headwinds.
forward_outlook: Management expects factory closures and elevated transit times to continue impacting product availability and revenue growth for the remainder of fiscal 2022, with most Vietnam factories anticipated to reopen in October requiring several months to return to full production. Transportation and logistics costs are expected to remain elevated throughout the fiscal year.
uncertainty_profile: Production recovery timing remains uncertain and subject to factors outside company control including government reopening approvals and factory ramp-up capabilities. Potential for new or prolonged factory closures, volatile inventory levels across markets, and evolving consumer behavior patterns present ongoing strategic adaptation requirements.
